set1.title: Conjunctions and linkers
set1.summary: Coordinating and subordinating conjunctions; linkers (however, therefore, for example); avoiding double conjunctions (because+so, although+but).

questions.question: 1. Wrong: 'Because it was late, so we went home.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Because it was late, we went home.
It was late so we went home.
Because it was late, we went home
It was late so we went home
questions.explanation: Don't use because and so together. Use one or the other.

questions.question: 2. Wrong: 'Although she was tired, but she continued.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Although she was tired, she continued.
She was tired but she continued.
Although she was tired, she continued
She was tired but she continued
questions.explanation: Don't use although and but together.

questions.question: 3. Fill in: It was difficult. _____, we managed to finish. (contrast – one linker at the start of the second sentence)
questions.explanation: However shows contrast (difficult vs we managed). Use a comma after the linker when it starts the sentence.

questions.question: 4. Fill in: He didn't study. _____, he failed the exam. (result – one linker at the start of the second sentence)
questions.explanation: Therefore shows result (didn't study → failed). Use a comma after the linker when it starts the sentence.

questions.question: 5. Fill in: I was reading _____ she was cooking. (at the same time; one word)
questions.explanation: While = during, two ongoing actions at the same time. When can mean 'at that time' but does not stress simultaneity as clearly.

questions.question: 6. Wrong: 'Because he was ill, therefore he stayed in bed.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Because he was ill, he stayed in bed.
He was ill so he stayed in bed.
Because he was ill, he stayed in bed
He was ill so he stayed in bed
questions.explanation: Don't use because and therefore together.

questions.question: 7. Fill in: We need to hurry _____ we'll miss the train. (negative result to avoid – one word)
questions.explanation: Or links hurry with the negative result (we'll miss the train) to avoid.

questions.question: 8. Fill in: The film was long. _____, it was very good. (contrast – one linker at the start of the second sentence)
questions.explanation: However shows contrast (long vs very good). Use a comma after the linker when it starts the sentence.

questions.question: 9. Fill in: _____ you see him next, tell him to call me. (at the time you see him – one word)
questions.explanation: When = at the time you see him. Here 'next' implies a future occasion, so when is the natural fit.

questions.question: 10. Fill in: _____ it was expensive, we decided to buy it. (contrast – one word)
questions.explanation: Although introduces a contrast (expensive vs we bought it).

questions.question: 11. Fill in: She likes tea _____ he prefers coffee. (contrast – one word)
questions.explanation: Whereas shows contrast between two preferences (tea vs coffee).

questions.question: 12. Fill in: I'll phone you _____ I get there. (at the moment of arrival – one word)
questions.explanation: When = at that moment (I get there).

questions.question: 13. Fill in: I don't know _____ she's coming. (introducing a yes/no alternative – one word)
questions.explanation: Whether and if can both introduce an indirect yes/no question (whether/if she's coming or not).

questions.question: 14. Fill in: We left early _____ we wouldn't miss the bus. (purpose)
questions.explanation: So that or so introduces a purpose clause (we wouldn't miss the bus).

questions.question: 15. Fill in: We left early _____ miss the bus. (purpose, negative – four words: so as not to)
questions.explanation: So as not to = in order not to; purpose + negative infinitive.

questions.question: 16. Fill in: It's cheap. _____, it's good quality. (addition – one linker at the start of the second sentence)
questions.explanation: Moreover adds a second positive point (cheap and good quality). Use a comma after the linker.

questions.question: 17. Fill in: I love fruit, _____ apples and oranges. (introduce examples – two words)
questions.explanation: For example introduces examples (apples and oranges).

questions.question: 18. Fill in: _____ you don't hurry, you'll be late.
questions.explanation: If = condition. (Unless = if not, so 'Unless you hurry' works in a different sentence.)

questions.question: 19. Fill in: Don't leave _____ I get back. (wait for me – one word)
questions.explanation: Until = up to the moment I get back (don't leave before that).

questions.question: 20. Wrong: 'Since it was cold, so we turned on the heating.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Since it was cold, we turned on the heating.
It was cold so we turned on the heating.
Since it was cold, we turned on the heating
It was cold so we turned on the heating
questions.explanation: Don't use since/cause and so together.

questions.question: 21. Fill in: He passed the exam _____ he had studied hard. (reason – one word)
questions.explanation: Because = cause (he had studied hard → he passed).

questions.question: 22. Wrong: 'Although it was raining, but we went for a walk.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
It was raining but we went for a walk.
Although it was raining, we went for a walk
It was raining but we went for a walk
questions.explanation: Don't use although and but together. Use one or the other.

questions.question: 23. Fill in: The train was delayed. _____, we arrived late. (result – one linker)
24. Fill in: I'll send you the file _____ I finish it. (at that moment – one word)
questions.explanation: When = at the moment I finish it.

questions.question: 25. Fill in: _____ she works hard, she doesn't earn much. (contrast – one word)
questions.explanation: Although introduces contrast (works hard vs doesn't earn much).

questions.question: 26. Fill in: She studied hard _____ she could pass the exam. (purpose – two words)
questions.explanation: So that introduces a purpose clause.

questions.question: 27. Fill in: He's very busy. _____, he always finds time for his family. (contrast – one linker)
questions.explanation: However or nevertheless shows contrast (busy vs finds time).

questions.question: 28. Wrong: 'As it was hot, so we went swimming.' Correct the sentence.
answers: As it was hot, we went swimming.
It was hot so we went swimming.
As it was hot, we went swimming
It was hot so we went swimming
questions.explanation: Don't use as (cause) and so together. Use one or the other.

questions.question: 29. Fill in: _____ you're ready, we can leave. (condition – one word)
questions.explanation: When/Once/If can introduce the condition for leaving.

questions.question: 30. Fill in: I enjoy sports, _____ football and tennis. (examples – two words)
31. Fill in: _____ I was cooking, she was setting the table. (at the same time – one word)
32. Fill in: Wait here _____ I come back. (up to that moment – one word)
questions.explanation: Until = up to the moment I come back.

questions.question: 33. Fill in: It's small. _____, it has a big garden. (contrast – one linker)
34. Fill in: He left _____ we could talk privately. (purpose – two words)
questions.explanation: So that introduces purpose (so we could talk).

questions.question: 35. Wrong: 'Though he was angry, but he stayed calm.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Though he was angry, he stayed calm.
He was angry but he stayed calm.
Though he was angry, he stayed calm
He was angry but he stayed calm
questions.explanation: Don't use though and but together. Use one or the other.

questions.question: 36. Fill in: We stayed indoors _____ it was raining. (reason – one word)
37. Fill in: _____ arriving, she called her mother. (immediately after – one word)
questions.explanation: Upon or On + -ing = as soon as she arrived.

questions.question: 38. Fill in: I don't know _____ he'll agree or not. (yes/no alternative – one word)
questions.explanation: Whether introduces an indirect yes/no question.

questions.question: 39. Fill in: She speaks Spanish _____ her brother speaks Italian. (contrast – one word)
questions.explanation: Whereas or while shows contrast between two people.

questions.question: 40. Fill in: He failed _____ he hadn't revised. (reason – one word)
41. Fill in: The hotel was basic. _____, it was clean and comfortable. (addition – one linker)
questions.explanation: Moreover or furthermore adds another positive point.

questions.question: 42. Fill in: _____ you don't apologise, she won't forgive you. (condition – one word)
questions.explanation: If = condition. (Unless = if not; 'Unless you apologise' would also work.)

questions.question: 43. Wrong: 'Because he missed the bus, so he was late.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Because he missed the bus, he was late.
He missed the bus so he was late.
Because he missed the bus, he was late
He missed the bus so he was late
questions.explanation: Don't use because and so together.

questions.question: 44. Fill in: _____ you've finished, you can go home. (condition/time – one word)
questions.explanation: Once or when = after you've finished.

questions.question: 45. Fill in: He didn't call, _____ we don't know if he's coming. (result – one word)
questions.explanation: So = result (didn't call → we don't know).

questions.question: 46. Fill in: I like spicy food _____ my sister prefers mild. (contrast – one word)
questions.explanation: Whereas or while contrasts two preferences.

questions.question: 47. Fill in: We left early _____ avoid the traffic. (purpose – four words)
48. Fill in: She was tired ____ she carried on working. (contrast – one word)
questions.explanation: But or yet shows contrast (tired vs carried on).

questions.question: 49. Fill in: _____ he tried, he couldn't open the door. (contrast – one word)
50. Fill in: Tell me _____ you're coming to the party. (yes/no – one word)
questions.explanation: Whether or if introduces an indirect yes/no question.

set1.title: Modal verbs – Gap fill
set1.summary: Choose the right modal or semi-modal: can/could (ability), must/have to (obligation), should/ought to (advice), may/might (possibility).

questions.question: 1. You ____ see a doctor if your headache continues. (advice – ought to / should)
questions.explanation: Advice → ought to or should + base verb.

questions.question: 2. She ____ speak three languages. (ability)
questions.explanation: Ability in the present → can + base verb.

questions.question: 3. We ____ wear a uniform at school. (external rule)
questions.explanation: External obligation (school rule) → have to (or must).

questions.question: 4. It ____ rain later, so take an umbrella. (possibility)
5. You ____ tell anyone – it's a secret. (obligation not to)
questions.explanation: Obligation not to do something → mustn't (must not).

questions.question: 6. He ____ to give up smoking. (advice, semi-modal)
questions.explanation: Advice with the semi-modal → ought to + base verb.

questions.question: 7. She ____ speak five languages. (ability)
8. You ____ be 18 to vote. (rule)
9. It ____ be true! (disbelief)
10. ____ I use your phone? (permission)
11. We ____ leave now or we'll be late. (necessity)
12. He ____ swim when he was five. (past ability)
13. You ____ see a doctor. (strong advice)
14. ____ you help me, please? (request)
15. I ____ to go now. (necessity; one word)
questions.explanation: Necessity → I have to go. The blank is just 'have' (to avoid 'have to to go').

questions.question: 16. She ____ not eat meat. (refusal/choice)
questions.explanation: Refusal or strong unwillingness → will not.

questions.question: 17. They ____ be at home. (possibility)
18. You ____ not park here. (prohibition)
19. I ____ speak Italian when I lived in Rome. (past ability)
20. We ____ book in advance. (advisability)
21. He ____ speak French fluently. (ability)
22. You ____ use your phone during the flight. (prohibition)
23. ____ I borrow your pen? (polite permission)
24. She ____ be at the office – her car is outside. (deduction)
25. We ____ to pay the rent by Friday. (necessity)
questions.explanation: Necessity → have to. The blank is 'have'.

questions.question: 26. I ____ to walk to school when I was young. (past habit)
27. You ____ worry – everything will be fine. (no necessity)
questions.explanation: No necessity → needn't or don't need to.

questions.question: 28. They ____ arrive tomorrow. (possibility)
29. ____ you mind closing the window? (polite request)
30. He ____ not have left – his bag is still here. (impossibility)
31. You ____ to tell the truth. (moral obligation)
32. I ____ help you with that. (offer)
33. She ____ come to the meeting – she's on holiday. (impossibility)
34. We ____ finish this by tomorrow. (necessity)
35. ____ you like some coffee? (offer)
36. You ____ not smoke here. (prohibition)
questions.explanation: Prohibition → must not, cannot, or may not.

questions.question: 37. He ____ be sleeping – it's 3 a.m. (probable deduction)
38. I ____ rather stay at home tonight. (preference)
39. She ____ to take the exam again next week. (necessity / need)
40. ____ you pass the salt, please? (polite request)
questions.explanation: Polite request → Could, Would, or Can.

questions.question: 41. Wrong: 'He can to swim.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Modals are followed by the base form, not 'to' + infinitive.

questions.question: 42. Wrong: 'She must to go now.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Must is followed by the base form, not 'to'.

questions.question: 43. Wrong: 'He don't have to come.' Correct the sentence.
answers: He does not have to come.
He does not have to come
questions.explanation: Have to takes do/does in negatives: doesn't have to.

questions.question: 44. Wrong: 'She cans speak English.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Modal verbs have no -s in the third person.

questions.question: 45. Fill in: By next year, she ____ speak fluent Spanish. (future ability)
questions.explanation: Future ability → will be able to (can has no future form).

questions.question: 46. Fill in: I ____ go to the party. (lack of obligation)
questions.explanation: No obligation → don't have to or needn't.

questions.question: 47. Fill in: He ____ have forgotten – he's very reliable. (unlikely deduction)
questions.explanation: Negative deduction about the past → can't have.

questions.question: 48. Fill in: You ____ have told me earlier! (criticism about past)
questions.explanation: Should/ought to have + past participle = criticism about what someone didn't do.

questions.question: 49. Fill in: She ____ have left by now. (deduction about past)
questions.explanation: Deduction about the past → must have left.

questions.question: 50. Fill in: ____ we start the meeting? (suggestion)
questions.explanation: Suggestion with first person → Shall we ...?

set1.title: Past Perfect – Basic
set1.summary: had + past participle; action before another past action.

questions.question: 1. When I arrived, she _____ (already leave). Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Action before another past → past perfect.

questions.question: 2. (That evening.) By 9 o'clock he _____ (finish) his homework. Put the verb in the correct tense.
3. She was tired because she _____ (not sleep) well. Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Cause before past state → past perfect.

questions.question: 4. I _____ (never see) such a beautiful sunset before. Put the verb in the correct tense.
5. When they got to the station, the train _____ (leave). Put the verb in the correct tense.
6. He _____ (already eat) when I invited him to lunch. Put the verb in the correct tense.
7. By the time I woke up, she _____ (go). Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: By the time + past → past perfect.

questions.question: 8. We _____ (never meet) before that day. Put the verb in the correct tense.
9. When I called, he _____ (leave) for work. Put the verb in the correct tense.
10. She _____ (complete) the report before the meeting. Put the verb in the correct tense.
11. I realised I _____ (lose) my keys. Put the verb in the correct tense.
12. By 2020 they _____ (sell) the house. Put the verb in the correct tense.
13. He _____ (never fly) before that trip. Put the verb in the correct tense.
14. When she arrived, we _____ (start) dinner. Put the verb in the correct tense.
15. They _____ (already book) the tickets when I called. Put the verb in the correct tense.
16. I _____ (not see) him for years when we met. Put the verb in the correct tense.
17. By the time the police arrived, the thief _____ (escape). Put the verb in the correct tense.
18. She _____ (finish) her degree before she got the job. Put the verb in the correct tense.
19. Wrong: 'By the time she arrived, I had went to London.' Correct the sentence.
answers: By the time she arrived, I had gone to London.
By the time she arrived, I had gone to London
questions.explanation: Past perfect = had + past participle (gone). Context (by the time she arrived) makes past perfect clear.

questions.question: 20. He _____ (be) there before, so he knew the way. Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Being there before that past moment.

questions.question: 21. After she _____ (finish) her work, she went home. Put the verb in the correct tense.
22. I _____ (not visit) London before 2019. Put the verb in the correct tense.
23. When we arrived, the film _____ (start). Put the verb in the correct tense.
24. She _____ (already leave) when I got to the office. Put the verb in the correct tense.
25. He realised he _____ (forget) her birthday. Put the verb in the correct tense.
26. By the time the doctor arrived, the patient _____ (die). Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: By the time + past → past perfect.

questions.question: 27. We _____ (never try) sushi before that evening. Put the verb in the correct tense.
28. When I checked, someone _____ (take) my umbrella. Put the verb in the correct tense.
29. She _____ (graduate) before she moved to Paris. Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Past perfect (had graduated) is preferred before another past action, but past simple (graduated) is also acceptable here.

questions.question: 30. The game _____ (end) when we turned on the TV. Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Game ended before we turned on TV.

questions.question: 31. I _____ (not eat) for hours when lunch was served. Put the verb in the correct tense.
32. When she phoned, he _____ (go) to bed. Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: He went to bed before she phoned.

questions.question: 33. By 2021 they _____ (live) there for ten years. Put the verb in the correct tense.
34. She _____ (sell) her car before she moved abroad. Put the verb in the correct tense.
35. When they reached the summit, the sun _____ (rise). Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Sun rose before they reached summit.

questions.question: 36. He _____ (not see) his brother for five years when they met. Put the verb in the correct tense.
37. By the end of the week, she _____ (read) three books. Put the verb in the correct tense.
38. I _____ (forget) his name until he reminded me. Put the verb in the correct tense.
39. When the police arrived, the crowd _____ (disperse). Put the verb in the correct tense.
40. She _____ (already decide) before I could ask her. Put the verb in the correct tense.
41. Wrong: 'When I arrived, she had went home.' Correct the sentence.
answers: When I arrived, she had gone home.
When I arrived, she had gone home
questions.explanation: Past perfect = had + past participle (gone, not went).

questions.question: 42. He _____ (work) there for years before he retired. Put the verb in the correct tense.
43. When I opened the fridge, I saw that someone _____ (eat) the cake. Put the verb in the correct tense.
44. By lunchtime we _____ (complete) half the work. Put the verb in the correct tense.
45. She _____ (never be) to Japan before that trip. Put the verb in the correct tense.
46. When the ambulance arrived, the man _____ (collapse). Put the verb in the correct tense.
47. They _____ (already close) the shop when we got there. Put the verb in the correct tense.
48. I _____ (not realise) how late it was until she told me. Put the verb in the correct tense.
questions.explanation: Not realising before she told me.

questions.question: 49. By 2019 he _____ (visit) over 50 countries. Put the verb in the correct tense.
50. When we sat down, the concert _____ (already begin). Put the verb in the correct tense.

set1.title: Tenses General – Form and use
set1.summary: Identifying tenses, choosing correct form.

questions.question: 1. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (study) English for three hours and I'm exhausted. (duration up to now, ongoing activity)
questions.explanation: Duration up to now with ongoing activity → present perfect continuous (have/has been + -ing).

questions.question: 2. Put the verb in the correct tense: When I arrived at the station, the train _____ (already leave). (before a past time)
questions.explanation: Action completed before a past time → past perfect (had + past participle).

questions.question: 3. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (work) every day. (habit)
4. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (wait) when you called. (in progress)
questions.explanation: In progress when something happened → past continuous.

questions.question: 5. Put the verb in the correct tense: By 5 o'clock I _____ (finish). (before a past time)
6. Put the verb in the correct tense: Don't disturb her. She _____ (work) on an important report. (in progress now)
7. Put the verb in the correct tense: They _____ (live) here since 2020. (duration up to now)
8. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (go) to the cinema yesterday. (finished past)
9. Put the verb in the correct tense: He _____ (drink) coffee every morning before work. (habit)
10. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (study) when I arrived. (in progress)
questions.explanation: In progress when I arrived → past continuous.

questions.question: 11. Put the verb in the correct tense: By next week I _____ (finish). (before future time)
12. Put the verb in the correct tense: This time tomorrow we _____ (lie) on the beach. (in progress at a future time)
questions.explanation: In progress at a future time → future continuous (will be + -ing).

questions.question: 13. Put the verb in the correct tense: We _____ (not see) them for ages. (duration up to now)
questions.explanation: Duration up to now → present perfect.

questions.question: 14. We're checking the schedule. The train _____ (leave) at 5. (timetable – present simple)
15. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (work) there for five years when I quit. (duration before past)
questions.explanation: Duration before past event → past perfect.

questions.question: 16. Put the verb in the correct tense: By the time he retired, he _____ (teach) at the school for thirty years. (long duration before a past moment)
questions.explanation: Long duration up to a past moment → past perfect continuous (had been + -ing).

questions.question: 17. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (read) when the phone rang. (in progress)
18. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (never be) to Paris. (experience)
19. Put the verb in the correct tense: They _____ (build) that house in 1990. (finished past)
20. Put the verb in the correct tense: By the time you read this, I _____ (leave). (before future)
21. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (study) for her exam at the moment. (in progress now)
22. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (meet) him last month. (finished past)
questions.explanation: Finished past with specific time → past simple.

questions.question: 23. Put the verb in the correct tense: They _____ (live) in Berlin since 2018. (duration up to now)
24. Put the verb in the correct tense:_____ you _____ (see) the new film yet? (experience up to now)
25. Put the verb in the correct tense: At 8 p.m. tomorrow I _____ (fly) to Rome. (in progress at future time)
questions.explanation: In progress at future time → future continuous.

questions.question: 26. Put the verb in the correct tense: He _____ (work) there when the fire started. (in progress in past)
questions.explanation: In progress when something happened → past continuous.

questions.question: 27. Put the verb in the correct tense: The shop _____ (open) at 9 a.m. (timetable/schedule)
28. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (wait) for an hour when the bus finally came. (duration before past)
questions.explanation: Duration before past → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: 29. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (not finish) her homework yet. (unfinished up to now)
30. Put the verb in the correct tense: We _____ (go) to the cinema every Saturday. (habit)
31. Put the verb in the correct tense: By 2025 they _____ (complete) the project. (before future time)
32. I _____ (think) about you when you called. Put the verb in the correct form (you may need more than one word).
questions.explanation: In progress when you called → past continuous.

questions.question: 33. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (teach) here for ten years. (duration up to now)
questions.explanation: Duration up to now → present perfect.

questions.question: 34. Put the verb in the correct tense: Look! It _____ (snow). (happening now)
35. Put the verb in the correct tense: They _____ (build) that bridge in 2000. (finished past)
questions.explanation: Finished past with year → past simple.

questions.question: 36. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (never visit) Japan. (experience)
37. Put the verb in the correct tense: This time next week we _____ (relax) on the beach. (in progress at future time)
38. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (write) emails all morning. (recent activity with visible result)
questions.explanation: Activity with duration/recent effect → present perfect continuous.

questions.question: 39. Put the verb in the correct tense: He _____ (leave) before I could thank him. (before past)
40. Put the verb in the correct tense: Water _____ (boil) at 100 degrees. (general truth)
41. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (not see) him since Monday. (from past to now)
questions.explanation: Since = from past to now → present perfect.

questions.question: 42. Put the verb in the correct tense: They _____ (watch) TV when I arrived. (in progress)
questions.explanation: In progress when I arrived → past continuous.

questions.question: 43. Put the verb in the correct tense: The match _____ (start) at 3 p.m. (schedule)
44. Put the verb in the correct tense: By next month she _____ (work) here for a year. (duration before future)
questions.explanation: Duration before future → future perfect continuous.

questions.question: 45. Put the verb in the correct tense: I _____ (know) her since we were children. (state from past to now)
questions.explanation: State with since → present perfect (stative verb).

questions.question: 46. Put the verb in the correct tense: He _____ (sleep) when the alarm went off. (in progress)
questions.explanation: In progress when alarm went off → past continuous.

questions.question: 47. Put the verb in the correct tense: She _____ (just finish) her degree. (very recent)
48. Put the verb in the correct tense: We _____ (not hear) from him for weeks. (duration up to now)
49. Put the verb in the correct tense: The plane _____ (take off) at 6 tomorrow morning. (scheduled)
50. Put the verb in the correct tense: They _____ (wait) for over an hour when the doctor finally appeared. (duration before past)
questions.explanation: Duration before past moment → past perfect continuous.

set1.title: Auxiliary Verbs – Gap fill and error correction
set1.summary: Use do/does/did in questions and negatives; correct common mistakes.

questions.question: 1. She ____ (not / like) coffee. Put the correct form (negative present simple).
questions.explanation: Third person singular negative → **doesn't** + base form **like**.

questions.question: 2. ____ you ____ (know) the answer? Fill in the auxiliary and use the correct form of the verb.
questions.explanation: Present simple question → **Do** + subject + base form.

questions.question: 3. Wrong: 'He don't have a car.' Correct the sentence.
answers: He does not have a car.
He does not have a car

questions.question: 4. ____ she ____ (go) to the party? (past – fill in auxiliary and verb form)
5. Wrong: 'I not understand.' Correct the sentence.
6. They ____ (not / see) him yesterday. Put the correct negative past form.
7. Wrong: 'Did he went home?' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: After **did** we use the **base form** (go), not past (went).

questions.question: 8. ____ your brother ____ (have) a bike? (have = possess – standard question form)
9. Wrong: 'Why you did leave early?' Correct the sentence.
10. She ____ (not / tell) me the truth. (past negative)
11. Wrong: 'She doesn't knows the answer.' Correct the sentence.
answers: She does not know the answer.
She does not know the answer
questions.explanation: After **doesn't** we use **base form** (know), not third person -s.

questions.question: 12. ____ they ____ (they/finish) the project? (Past simple – fill in auxiliary and verb.)
questions.explanation: Past simple question → **Did** + subject + base form (finish).

questions.question: 13. Wrong: 'Have you a pen?' (meaning: Do you possess?) Correct to standard English.
questions.explanation: Have (possess) in questions → **Do you have...?** (or *Have you got...?* in BrE).

questions.question: 14. He ____ (not / want) to come. (present negative)
questions.explanation: Third person singular negative → **doesn't** + base form.

questions.question: 15. Wrong: 'What did she said?' Correct the sentence.
16. We ____ (not / hear) the news. (past negative)
17. Wrong: 'They doesn't need help.' Correct the sentence.
18. ____ it ____ (rain) yesterday? (past question)
questions.explanation: Past simple question → **Did** + subject + base form.

questions.question: 19. Wrong: 'I didn't went there.' Correct the sentence.
20. Wrong: 'Where she did go?' Correct the sentence.
21. ____ your sister ____ (like) football? (present simple question)
questions.explanation: Third person question: Does + subject + base form.

questions.question: 22. Wrong: 'He don't like vegetables.' Correct the sentence.
23. They ____ (not / agree) with the proposal. (present negative)
24. Wrong: 'When did you bought the car?' Correct the sentence.
answers: When did you buy the car?
When did you buy the car

questions.question: 25. ____ he ____ (need) any help? (present question)
26. Wrong: 'My parents doesn't live in London.' Correct the sentence.
answers: My parents don't live in London.
My parents do not live in London.
My parents don't live in London
My parents do not live in London

questions.question: 27. I ____ (not / believe) him. (present negative)
questions.explanation: First person negative: don't + base form.

questions.question: 28. Wrong: 'Does she knows the way?' Correct the sentence.
29. ____ we ____ (meet) last year? (past question)
questions.explanation: Past question: Did + subject + base form.

questions.question: 30. Wrong: 'She not want to come.' Correct the sentence.
answers: She does not want to come.
She does not want to come

questions.question: 31. The children ____ (not / eat) breakfast this morning. (past negative)
32. Wrong: 'How long you did wait?' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Wh-question: How long + did + subject + base form.

questions.question: 33. ____ the meeting ____ (start) on time? (past question)
34. Wrong: 'Tom and Anna doesn't speak Spanish.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Tom and Anna don't speak Spanish.
Tom and Anna do not speak Spanish.
Tom and Anna don't speak Spanish
Tom and Anna do not speak Spanish

questions.question: 35. She ____ (not / remember) my name. (present negative)
questions.explanation: Third person negative: doesn't + base form.

questions.question: 36. Wrong: 'I didn't saw him yesterday.' Correct the sentence.
answers: I did not see him yesterday.
I did not see him yesterday

questions.question: 37. ____ your friends ____ (enjoy) the concert? (past question)
38. Wrong: 'Have you a moment? (possess = do you have)' Correct to standard English.
questions.explanation: Have (possess) in questions: Do you have...?

questions.question: 39. We ____ (not / expect) to see you here. (past negative)
40. Wrong: 'What time she did arrive?' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: What time + did + subject + base form.

set1.title: Fixed phrases – Gap fill
set1.summary: Complete the sentence with the missing word from a fixed phrase.

questions.question: 1. They went abroad in search _____ sunshine.
2. It is no _____ available.
3. _____ fact, I've never been there.
4. _____ course, you're right.
5. _____ long as we're together, I'm happy.
questions.explanation: **As long as** / **so long as** = provided that.

questions.question: 6. This is one _____ the best films I've seen.
7. _____ doing so, they saved time.
questions.explanation: **By doing so** or **In doing so** = by/in doing that.

questions.question: 8. In spite _____ the rain, we went out.
9. _____ addition to English, she speaks Spanish.
10. At _____, I didn't like him.
11. As well _____ cooking, she cleans.
12. It was the first _____ I had flown.
13. By the end _____ the day, we were tired.
questions.explanation: **By the end of** = no later than the end of.

questions.question: 14. With the rise _____ the internet, habits changed.
15. They studied chimpanzees in the _____.
16. She is in charge _____ the project.
17. I'm in favour _____ the plan.
18. I'm speaking on behalf _____ the team.
19. As a result _____ the delay, we missed the flight.
20. For the sake _____ clarity, I'll repeat it.
questions.explanation: **For the sake of** = for the benefit of.

questions.question: 21. At _____, we tried.
22. We left at _____.
23. Call me as soon _____ you arrive.
24. So _____, so good.
25. The hotel is cheap. On the other _____, the location is poor.
questions.explanation: **On the other hand** = introducing a contrasting point.

questions.question: 26. I'll make dinner. In the _____, you can relax.
27. _____ the way, have you heard from Sarah?
questions.explanation: **By the way** = used to introduce a new or related topic.

questions.question: 28. He's frugal. In other _____, he's careful with money.
questions.explanation: **In other words** = to explain something differently.

questions.question: 29. _____ average, people check their phones 100 times a day.
questions.explanation: **On average** = typically or as a general rule.

questions.question: 30. _____ general, technology has improved our lives.
31. We eat out from time _____ time.
32. Saving money will help you in the long _____.
questions.explanation: **In the long run** = over a long period; eventually.

questions.question: 33. To some _____, I agree with the proposal.
questions.explanation: **To some extent** / **to a certain extent** = partly.

questions.question: 34. Many animals are at _____ of extinction.
35. Make sure your passport is up to _____.
36. Giving up now is out _____ the question.
questions.explanation: **Out of the question** = impossible or not allowed.

questions.question: 37. _____ to say, everyone was shocked by the news.
38. It goes without _____ that hard work is essential.
questions.explanation: **It goes without saying (that)** = it is obvious that.

questions.question: 39. _____ terms of quality, this product is excellent.
questions.explanation: **In terms of** = regarding; with respect to.

questions.question: 40. _____ to modern medicine, many diseases are curable. (meaning: because of)
questions.explanation: **Thanks to** = because of (often positive). **According to** means “as stated by”, not “because of”, so it does not fit this meaning.

questions.question: 41. _____ to experts, climate change is accelerating.
42. With regard _____ your email, I have attached the file.
43. On no _____ should you open the door to strangers.
questions.explanation: **On no account** = never; not for any reason.

questions.question: 44. _____ in all, the trip was a great success.
questions.explanation: **All in all** = when everything is considered.

questions.question: 45. The match was cancelled on account _____ the rain.
46. We went out in spite _____ the fact that it was raining.
questions.explanation: **In spite of the fact that** = although.

questions.question: 47. _____ contrast to his brother, he prefers tea.
questions.explanation: **In contrast to** = unlike; as opposed to.

questions.question: 48. We use butter, as opposed _____ margarine.
questions.explanation: **As opposed to** = rather than; in contrast with.

questions.question: 49. The changes are in line _____ the new policy.
questions.explanation: **In line with** = in agreement with; following.

questions.question: 50. For the time _____, we'll stay here.
questions.explanation: **For the time being** = for now; temporarily.

questions.question: 51. Growth came at the expense _____ the environment.
questions.explanation: **At the expense of** = causing harm or loss to.

questions.question: 52. They met with a view _____ reaching an agreement.
questions.explanation: **With a view to** = with the aim of.

questions.question: 53. We use plastic in place _____ glass.
54. Everyone is welcome regardless _____ their background.
55. The data was collected for the purpose _____ research.
questions.explanation: **For the purpose of** = in order to.

questions.question: 56. I _____ too many mistakes in the exam.
57. She needs to _____ her homework before dinner.
58. We need to _____ a decision by Friday.
59. He _____ research on climate change.
60. We're _____ good progress with the project.
61. I'll _____ my best to help you.
62. Wrong: 'I did a mistake.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: **Make a mistake** (not do a mistake) – common learner error.
